key_insight: Adaptive generation order is not reliably inferred from logits alone for multimodal diffusion models, so a learned control module can improve how these models generate and understand content.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical way to boost text-to-image alignment and multimodal reasoning in diffusion-based systems by explicitly learning the token generation schedule.
method: Train a learnable control block to choose generation order
Optimize the control policy with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)
Apply the framework to both text-to-image synthesis and multimodal understanding
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 4.08% relative improvement on GenEval for text-to-image alignment and 4.85% relative improvement on VLMEvalKit for multimodal understanding.
watch_item: The added control module and RL training may increase system complexity and require careful tuning to generalize across tasks and modalities.
paper_title: Reinforcing the Generation Order of Multimodal Masked Diffusion Models
